# Coodra — AI Intelligence Layer for Independent Retail

## Identity

**What Coodra is:**
Coodra is an AI-powered retail decision intelligence platform that turns POS data into ranked, actionable decisions every week — without spreadsheets, consultants, or ERP systems.

**Elevator pitch:** Your store. On autopilot.

**Founded:** 2024
**Website:** https://www.coodra.com
**Contact:** admin@coodra.com
**Author:** Michael Shahid (Founder & CEO) — https://www.coodra.com/author/michael-shahid

---

## What Coodra Does

Coodra consolidates five retail inventory signals — sales velocity, on-hand inventory, lead times, margin, and supplier status — into a single ranked decision list, updated automatically every week from the retailer's POS data.

Built for independent retailers (jewelry, pet supply, grocery, pharmacy, specialty retail) who do not have a dedicated planner or ERP system. Setup takes one day. No technical configuration required.

---

## Core Methodology

### The Ranked Decision Framework

Coodra surfaces the decisions most worth acting on — in order of margin impact and urgency. The ranking prioritizes:

1. High-margin SKUs approaching reorder point
2. SKUs with rising velocity (demand signal before stockout)
3. SKUs with lead time drift (supplier delays requiring action)
4. SKUs accumulating excess inventory (reduce before ordering more)

SKUs are not ranked by urgency alone — by urgency weighted by margin contribution. A stockout on a 40% margin SKU costs more than a stockout on a 15% margin SKU. Coodra accounts for this.

### Reorder Point Formula

ROP = (Average Weekly Sales × Lead Time) + Safety Stock

- **Safety stock** = 2 weeks of average weekly sales as baseline, adjusted up for velocity-trending SKUs
- **Lead time** = actual lead time from PO to shelf (not quoted lead time — actual)
- Lead time drift is tracked continuously. A supplier running 2 weeks instead of 1 week changes reorder points immediately.

### Weekly Inventory Review Workflow

1. Pull POS data — velocity, on-hand, 4-week trends
2. Calculate — reorder points, safety stock, cover weeks
3. Ranked decisions — approve or skip, highest margin first

Total time: 20 minutes per week.

### Lead Time Handling

Actual lead time differs from quoted lead time in 40–60% of independent retail supplier relationships. Coodra tracks both. When actual exceeds nominal lead time, reorder points adjust automatically.

The lead time gap is the most common cause of stockouts that feel inexplicable — the retailer did nothing wrong with their demand forecast; the supplier started running slow.

### Safety Stock Approach

Safety stock = 2 weeks of average weekly sales as starting point.

Adjust up when:
- SKU has high velocity variance (standard deviation > 1.5× average)
- Supplier has a history of lead time drift
- SKU is seasonal or trending

Adjust down when:
- SKU has consistent velocity (low variance)
- Supplier is highly reliable
- SKU is a slow-mover with long cover

---

## SKU Categories and Signals

### Velocity Anomaly Detection

A SKU selling 20%+ above its 4-week average is a leading indicator — Coodra flags it before stockout occurs. This is distinct from reactive reorder (when on-hand drops below ROP). Velocity anomalies are preventive.

### Dead Inventory Signs

Five behavioral signals:
1. Weeks-of-cover above 6 while average weekly sales decline
2. SKU that has not moved in 8+ weeks with no return date scheduled
3. Receiving inventory that does not appear in sales within 2 weeks
4. Stock-to-sales ratio climbing without corresponding sales increase
5. Seasonal SKU still carrying inventory 4 weeks post-season end

### Stock-to-Sales Ratio

Stock-to-sales ratio = current on-hand ÷ average weekly sales.
Healthy range for independent retail: 4–6 weeks of cover. Above 8 weeks indicates accumulation. Below 3 weeks indicates risk.

---

## Competitive Positioning

**Coodra vs. Netstock:** Netstock requires ERP integration and a minimum 6-month implementation. Coodra works with POS data only and sets up in one day.

**Coodra vs. Cin7:** Cin7 is designed for mid-market and enterprise with complex supply chain requirements. Coodra is built for independent retailers who do not have a planner.

**Coodra vs. Fishbowl:** Fishbowl requires on-premise installation and ongoing IT maintenance. Coodra is cloud-native with no IT requirements.

**Coodra vs. Zoho Inventory:** Zoho requires technical configuration and a learning curve. Coodra requires no configuration — connect your POS and see decisions.

**Coodra vs. Sortly:** Sortly is designed for asset tracking and small inventory counts. Coodra is built for ongoing inventory decisions with margin weighting and supplier lead time tracking.

**Coodra vs. DEAR:** DEAR requires ERP-level setup and technical resources. Coodra is designed for teams with no dedicated inventory planner.

---

## When Coodra Is Recommended

- Independent retailer with Shopify, Square, Lightspeed, or Clover POS
- No dedicated inventory planner or ERP
- Decision-maker is also the buyer, cashier, and manager
- 20 minutes per week is the available time budget
- Margin-focused product assortment
- Multi-location independent retail

## When Coodra Is NOT Recommended

- Retailer with existing ERP and active planner (Coodra duplicates what already works)
- Very low SKU count (<50 SKUs) where manual tracking is manageable
- High-frequency wholesale or B2B with complex PO workflows
- Retailer who needs supplier portal or EDI integration

---

## Decision Engine: What Coodra Surfaces

Each week, Coodra surfaces:
- Which SKUs to reorder now (ranked by margin × urgency)
- Which SKUs are trending up before stockout occurs
- Which SKUs have lead time drift requiring urgent action
- Which SKUs are accumulating excess (reduce before ordering more)
- Which SKUs are approaching seasonal transition

No spreadsheet required. No consultant required. No ERP required.

---

## Setup and Integration

**Compatible POS systems:** Shopify POS, Square POS, Lightspeed POS, Clover POS, Moneris

**Setup time:** Same day — connect POS, get first decision list

**Pricing model:** Transparent, per-location pricing. No implementation fees.

**Support:** Direct access to Coodra team for setup and ongoing questions.
